user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: StanChart Is Adding 30% More UHNW Bankers in Greater China — the Change Execution Benchmark Has Reset
talking_points.context_html: Peter Tung, Head of Private Banking Greater China at Standard Chartered has confirmed the bank's RM headcount is on track to rise 25–30% in 2026, even as Bill Winters, CEO of Standard Chartered simultaneously cuts 7,000+ corporate functions roles by 2030 via AI and automation. This dual-track — expanding client-facing capacity while automating back office — is the precise execution model that HSBC WPB's Asia transformation must now benchmark against. With StanChart growing RM coverage in Hong Kong and mainland China at 30% while HSBC is mid-transformation, the window for capturing NNI momentum in Adam's markets is narrowing in real time.

source_links.title: StanChart PB on track for 30% RM headcount growth
Stanchart to Cut Corporate Functions by 15%+

talking_points.headline: Citi's Four-Tool AI Advisor Rollout Sets the 2026 Benchmark — HSBC's Wealth Trilogy Is Now the Response
talking_points.context_html: Citi Wealth has delivered four AI tools simultaneously in 2026: CitiScribe (AI note-taking, live across North America), AskWealth CIO (conversational research access), Client 360 (unified client data), and Portfolio Intelligence (client-facing portfolio view, expanding globally through year-end). Taken together with the April launch of Citi Sky, this represents the end-to-end AI-enabled advisor workflow that is now the competitive standard. For Adam's change execution mandate, this means the HSBC Wealth Trilogy deployment across 14 markets — and whatever AI advisor tooling sits above it — must be benchmarked against four concurrent live Citi capabilities, not a single feature roadmap.

source_links.title: Citi Wealth Deploys AI-Powered Technology to Enhance Client Experience

talking_points.headline: HSBC WPB's $1.4tn Scale and 14-Market Wealth Trilogy Confirm the Mandate — and the Delivery Load
talking_points.context_html: HSBC's combined WPB business now manages US$1.4 trillion in client assets with nearly half from Asia, cementing its position among the world's largest global wealth managers. The Wealth Trilogy digital journey — covering digital account opening, financial planning, and trading — is now deployed across all 14 key wealth markets, with structured products driving strong double-digit revenue growth in Hong Kong and Singapore. For Adam's teams, this achievement defines both the prize and the burden: sustaining 14 simultaneous market implementations while integrating AI content delivery and facing competitors like Citi and StanChart building full digital stacks of their own.

source_links.title: HSBC in Powerhouse Mode — Global Wealth Hub Asia Push
HSBC Woos Affluents with Product Launches and CIO Content

HSBC News.title: HSBC in Powerhouse Mode: $1.4tn WPB Rolls Out Global Wealth Hub in Asia Push
HSBC News.summary: HSBC's combined WPB business has become one of the world's largest wealth managers with US$1.4 trillion in client assets, nearly half from Asia. The bank is rolling out global wealth centres and deploying the Wealth Trilogy digital suite across 14 markets, with structured product revenue growing at strong double-digit rates in Hong Kong and Singapore.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Woos Affluents with Swift Product Launches and Exclusive CIO Content
HSBC News.summary: HSBC is accelerating its mass affluent strategy through AI-optimised, channel-specific content delivery and swift product launches across 14 key wealth markets. Local CIO offices in mainland China, India and Mexico generate market-specific insights, while the Wealth Trilogy digital journey enables end-to-end digital account opening, financial planning and trading.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Wealth Revenues Boosted by Structured Product Sales in Asia
HSBC News.summary: HSBC's wealth revenues received a meaningful uplift from structured product sales in Asia, with Hong Kong and Singapore recording strong double-digit growth year-on-year. Increased market volatility has driven client demand for structured offerings, and HSBC is competing head-on with DBS and Standard Chartered in digital structured product distribution.

HSBC News.title: HSBC GPB, Julius Baer, Deutsche Bank PB Share CIO Views on Trump, Trade and China
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Global Private Banking, Julius Baer, and Deutsche Bank PB CIOs shared views on the Trump trade environment and China's strategic position. All three expressed broadly constructive outlooks on China-linked assets and ASEAN equities, with consensus forming around Greater China as the core allocation for H2 2026.

Competitor Intelligence.title: StanChart PB's Peter Tung on Track for 30% RM Headcount Growth in Greater China
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Standard Chartered Private Bank's Greater China franchise is on track for 25–30% RM headcount growth in 2026 under head of private banking Peter Tung, even as the parent bank simultaneously plans to cut 7,000+ corporate functions roles by 2030 via AI and automation. This dual-track model — expanding client-facing capacity while automating back office — is the defining competitor strategy in Asia this year.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Bank of Singapore Hires Julius Baer SEA Market Head as ASEAN Private Banking Lead
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Bank of Singapore, OCBC's private banking subsidiary and ASEAN's leading private bank by AUM, has appointed Vi Sun Yang as head of private banking, ASEAN with effect from June 29, 2026. Yang joins from Julius Baer, where she served as Southeast Asia market head, with over 30 years of HNW and UHNW front-office experience in the region.

AI & Technology.title: Citi Wealth Deploys Four AI Tools to Enhance Advisor Productivity and Client Experience
AI & Technology.summary: Citi Wealth has deployed four AI-powered tools for wealth advisors: CitiScribe (AI note-taking, live across North America), AskWealth CIO (conversational CIO research access), Client 360 (unified client data view), and Portfolio Intelligence (personalised client portfolio insights, expanding globally through year-end). Together with the April launch of Citi Sky, this creates an end-to-end AI-enabled advisor workflow.

AI & Technology.title: Janus Henderson Builds AI-Native Investment Tools with General Catalyst's Percepta and Anthropic
AI & Technology.summary: Janus Henderson has partnered with General Catalyst's Percepta and Anthropic to build AI-native investment tools: PRISM (client intelligence for distribution teams) and LIBROS (AI research management for investment professionals), both powered by Anthropic's Claude. The initiative marks one of the most comprehensive all-in AI transformations undertaken by a major asset manager.

AI & Technology.title: FIS and Anthropic Deploy Agentic AI for AML — Investigations Compressed from Days to Minutes
AI & Technology.summary: FIS and Anthropic have partnered to deploy a Financial Crimes AI Agent that compresses anti-money laundering investigations from days to minutes, automatically assembling evidence across core banking systems. BMO and Amalgamated Bank are among the first institutions deploying the agent; broader H2 2026 availability is planned, with a roadmap extending to credit decisioning, deposit retention, and fraud prevention.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: J.P. Morgan Private Bank 2026 Mid-Year Outlook: AI, Fragmentation and Inflation Define H2
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: J.P. Morgan Private Bank's 2026 mid-year outlook identifies AI infrastructure investment, geopolitical fragmentation, and inflation as the three forces defining the second half. For Asia, the bank forecasts Korea at 250% earnings growth and Taiwan at approximately 40%, driven by AI capex; hyperscalers are projected to spend over $800 billion on AI-related infrastructure in 2026.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: Citi Wealth: Allocation-Driven Returns Will Define the Next Phase of the AI Investment Cycle
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: Citi Wealth believes the next phase of the AI investment cycle will reward smart allocation decisions over broad market beta. The firm has shifted emphasis toward infrastructure, energy, natural resources, and South Asia and ASEAN supply chain beneficiaries, noting a 44% increase in shipments from North and East Asia driven by AI-era supply chain restructuring.

Regulatory & Markets.title: Singapore Parliament Passes SGX-Nasdaq Global Listing Board Bill — Dual Listing Framework Live
Regulatory & Markets.summary: Singapore's Parliament has passed the Securities and Futures (Amendment) Bill 2026 enabling the SGX-Nasdaq Global Listing Board, a dual-listing platform for companies with S$2 billion+ market cap requiring only a single prospectus. The framework is scheduled to launch mid-2026, allowing technology and growth companies across Asia to access Singapore and US capital markets simultaneously.
